title: PreToolUse spawn guard: rostered persistent Teammates must not be spawned as one-shot background agents
description: Brooks's directive: the Team Lead keeps spawning rostered persistent Teammates (builder, scribe, viewer...) via the one-shot Agent tool as background agents, despite the TEAM_DIRECTIVE routing rule. Add a spawn_guard hook feature (on the existing toggle plumbing): PreToolUse on the Agent/Task spawn tool reads the requested agent type; if it matches a rostered persistent-Teammate type being spawned as a transient/background one-shot, deny with a reason naming the routing rule and the right mechanism. The persistent-types list is per-Instance config (hooks.config.json is already variant-classed — natural home). Invoked Agent types (gates, clerk, nothing-new) pass untouched. Default: off in template, on in this Instance (validation precedent). Fail-safe contract holds; subject to the new CODE_STANDARDS hook-logging rule.
acceptance_criteria: Spawning a configured persistent-Teammate type via the one-shot spawn tool is denied with a reason citing the TEAM_DIRECTIVE routing rule
Toggle off in template, on in this Instance; missing/malformed config no-ops
Invocations logged per the CODE_STANDARDS ## Hooks rule
fix: spawn-guard PreToolUse hook denies one-shot spawns of configured persistent-Teammate types citing the TEAM_DIRECTIVE routing rule; per-Instance type list in variant hooks.config.json; off in template, on here. Gates PASS.
